name: Quebec Law 25 (Act Respecting the Protection of Personal Information in the Private Sector)
summary: Quebec's comprehensive modernization of its private-sector privacy framework, introducing GDPR-style requirements in three phases completed by September 2024. It applies to all organizations collecting, holding, or using personal information of Quebec residents, regardless of where the organization is based.
keyRequirements: Designate a person responsible for the protection of personal information
Mandatory privacy impact assessments for information systems and electronic service delivery
Breach notification to the Commission d'acces a l'information and affected individuals
Right to data portability in a commonly used technological format
Express consent required for the collection of sensitive personal information
Privacy by default for technology products and services
Restrictions on profiling and automated decision-making with right to explanation
penalties: Administrative monetary penalties up to $10 million CAD or 2% of worldwide turnover. Penal fines up to $25 million CAD or 4% of worldwide turnover for serious offenses. Private right of action for individuals.
